title: Collective Electronic Entanglement via Infrared Cavity-Induced Vibronic Transduction
abstract: Polaritonic architectures seek to engineer molecular properties by hybridizing localized degrees of freedom with delocalized optical cavity fields. However, scaling laws impose a severe bottleneck on N-molecule collective strong coupling: because each molecule contributes only a fractional share to the collective state, localized responses undergo O(1/N) ensemble dilution. We demonstrate a violation of this scaling using fluorescence-encoded infrared spectroscopy of molecular ensembles under vibrational strong coupling, where macroscopically synchronized electronic responses scale as O(1). This scale-invariance reveals a regime of vibronic quantum transduction, where non-local vibrational entanglement is translated into collective electronic entanglement. By demonstrating the generation of macroscopically entangled electronic states from vibro-polaritons without an O(1/N) penalty, these results provide a scalable framework for room-temperature quantum technologies and coherent steering of non-adiabatic chemical pathways.

bite.hook: Infrared cavity coupling bypassed the standard 1/N dilution limit, enabling scale-invariant O(1) molecular entanglement.
tldr: Investigated how to generate scalable collective electronic entanglement across molecular ensembles inside optical cavities.
Used fluorescence-encoded infrared spectroscopy on molecular ensembles undergoing vibrational strong coupling.
Bypassed the usual 1/N ensemble dilution penalty, achieving scale-invariant O(1) electronic entanglement.
bite.whyItMatters: Quantum effects typically fade fast when spread across large groups of molecules. Overcoming this bottleneck enables room-temperature quantum technologies and precise optical control over chemical reactions.
bite.eli5: Normally, trying to share a single quantum connection across a huge crowd dilutes the effect until everyone gets almost nothing. This team found a trick using light mirrors to keep every molecule strongly entangled, no matter how big the crowd gets.

keyNumbers.label: observed scaling
keyNumbers.context: scale-invariant collective electronic response achieved without dilution

keyNumbers.label: standard scaling limit
keyNumbers.context: conventional dilution penalty expected for N-molecule ensembles

glossary.shortDef: A state where light inside a cavity strongly hybridizes with molecular vibrational modes.
glossary.eli5Def: Trapping light and molecular motion together so tightly they merge into a new hybrid state.

glossary.shortDef: A hybrid quasiparticle formed by strong coupling between photons and material excitations.
glossary.eli5Def: A particle created by blending a light wave with atomic movement.

glossary.shortDef: The conversion of quantum information from one physical form or frequency to another.
glossary.eli5Def: Translating a quantum message from one form of energy into another without losing the signal.
